frame_3_role: Masthead · self-describing inventory · the entry-point for any auditor
description: Self-describing inventory of every file synced to wallet-verdict-ledger today. Every file has size + sha256 + role. Cross-references the day's OTS-anchored digest so the manifest itself is tamper-detectable: if any synced file changes after publication, its sha256 won't match this manifest record. The manifest IS the receipt-of-receipts.

truth_vs_derived_doctrine.rule: TRUTH-LEDGER · this ledger captures verifiable inputs (wallet trades · orderbook · settlements · counterparty · UMA) · NOT our predictions. Within truth: NON-BACKFILLABLE files are the #1 moat · backfillable files are auxiliary.
truth_vs_derived_doctrine.why: Per operator directive 2026-05-10 mid-PM HKT: "we set up a ledger to store something to validate the correctness of wallet and orderbook, the truth, not to record we own prediction · no one care about our prediction · timestamp it · encry it is no use". v60cq sharpening: "the trick is the most important moat is some truth that cannot be backfilled, this is #1 moat. we can still store other important data (which can be backfilled) but top priority is those cannot be."
truth_vs_derived_doctrine.sp_parallel: S&P's moat is the bond default-rate dataset they curate · not the ratings they issue. VERDICT mirrors that pattern: the wallet+orderbook truth dataset is the moat · our scores are downstream evidence. Among truth files: ephemeral observations (orderbook at trade-time · forward-witnessed timestamps) are the moat-of-moats since competitors cannot reconstruct them retroactively.
truth_vs_derived_doctrine.ots_anchor_scope: Truth-files only (methodology L4-truth-anchor v2.0) · derived files (vpi_nav · vri · backtest · derived_metrics · brier_skill · venue_rating) are NO LONGER part of the OTS digest input set as of v60cp · they remain in the public ledger but unanchored. All TRUTH files (ephemeral + permanent) are in the digest equally · but the ephemeral subset is what no entrant on day N+365 can ever clone.
truth_vs_derived_doctrine.why_ephemeral_is_top_priority: A competitor arriving on day N+365 with infinite compute and access to all public sources CAN re-derive: wallet trades (Polymarket Subgraph) · settlement attestations (Polygon RPC chain history) · counterparty graph (on-chain tx) · UMA oracle resolutions (UMA chain). They CANNOT reconstruct: orderbook book state at trade-time (only existed during the 60s snapshotter window) · wallet_microstructure_book (joined book+trades at the moment) · audit_ledger forward-witnessed timestamps (their git history doesn't include our day-N commits) · J11/J12 observable features captured at observation-time. THESE are the structural-moat files · the ones we should defend, prioritize, and compound first.
truth_vs_derived_doctrine.how_to_use: Anyone can clone this repo · sha256-verify any TRUTH file against this manifest · run `ots verify` on the engine receipt · and re-derive their own scores from the same truth. Our scores are one valid derivation; theirs may differ; the truth doesn't change.

manifest_self_anchor.note: OTS-anchored separately from the engine-state digest. The engine anchor covers the 9 core data files; the manifest anchor covers THIS manifest (which itself sha256s every file in the ledger). Together they form the double-anchor pattern · two independent Bitcoin receipts per day.

three_layer_architecture.layer_1_github: public ledger / readable history · wallet-verdict-ledger repo
three_layer_architecture.layer_2_ots: decentralized timestamp proof · daily Bitcoin anchor (2 receipts: engine + manifest)
three_layer_architecture.layer_3_polygon: optional easy enterprise receipt / settlement proof · W3 settlement attestations live · daily-hash contract DEFERRED until enterprise pull
three_layer_architecture.rule: Do NOT put bulk data on-chain · only hashes
three_layer_architecture.reopen_when: Watchtower customer asks for L1/L2 receipt explicitly · OR regulator subpoena hypothetical · OR GitHub takes visible action against similar product

verification_steps: Step 2 · for each entry in synced_files · run `sha256sum <path>` · compare to .sha256
Step 3 · run `ots verify data/ots_receipts/<date>.digest.ots` to confirm L4 Bitcoin anchor
Step 4 · for each entry in strategy_locks · sha256(canonical_text) matches · proves positioning was X on date Y
